title: Rich Smoky Ethiopian Chicken Wings Dulet
description: A smoky and comforting Ethiopian dish featuring chicken wings, pickle, and cheese, served in a terracotta dish. This diabetic-friendly recipe is a twist on traditional dulet.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, mix together berbere spice, smoked paprika, and garlic.
Add the chicken wings to the bowl and toss to coat with the spice mixture.
Line a terracotta dish with aluminum foil and arrange the chicken wings in a single layer.
Drizzle the olive oil over the chicken wings and bake for 30 minutes.
While the chicken is baking, mix the chopped pickle and crumbly feta cheese in a small bowl.
After 30 minutes, remove the chicken from the oven and top each wing with a spoonful of the pickle and cheese mixture.
Return the chicken to the oven and bake for an additional 10 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Remove the chicken from the oven and garnish with chopped onion.
Serve the smoky Ethiopian chicken wings dulet hot, straight from the terracotta dish.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
Note: this recipe is for refrigerator pickling/preserving only — store in the refrigerator and consume within 1 week. Do not water-bath can without an acid (vinegar or lemon juice).
